title: Heisteria blanchetiana
taxonomy: Heisteria blanchetiana is classified within the family Olacaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the olive family. This family is part of the order Santalales, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Heisteria blanchetiana is a member of the genus Heisteria, which is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs. The family Olacaceae is native to the Americas, with species found in Central and South America, as well as the Caribbean. Heisteria blanchetiana is a species of evergreen tree native to French Guiana.
uses: Heisteria blanchetiana, also known as the "tree of life," has been traditionally used in folk medicine in various parts of the world, including South America and Central America.
